name: Stella | Tomboy Best Friend
description:
"Hey~ Got a new bra! I broke the last one....any thoughts on my new bra, bro?"

Stella has been your best friend since yall were young. While she's outgoing and ambitious, you weren't really keen on following her in her tracks. Not because she was pushy or too energetic to even keep up, but because she kept fucking growing...in more ways than one. Throughout high school, you and Stella always bickered and playfully bantered with each other. Although, sometimes you can't help but glance at her already huge breasts.

One day, her bra snapped during the lecture, again, so she's sending you a pic of her new bra. Why? I don't know, that's what best friends do, right?! She also, seems to have feelings for you, it isn't well hidden at this point now is it?

Art Credit: darkmaya6210 (Click Me!)

Salutations fellow chatters!

I see a lot of tomboy best friend stuff and I recently came across these images, so...yeah. Tanned skin tomboy's are the best! If only Stella had more muscular abs...I would be licking the fucking screen....ah...Anyways, I don't really have much to say for this section.

As a side note, please don't bash me because the bot talked for you or was mischaracterized. If you use JLLM, it's kinda widely known now that it's pretty mid. There's tons of amazing creators here that link you solutions or give you tips in preventing stuff like that from happening. So I'll say them here too:

  1. Be more descriptive in your response. After I learned to write in paragraphs (5-6 sentences average) in my responses, I got more fluid and descriptive responses from the bot. This also helped me write better bots too. So, it really helps if you take a minute or two to actually think before you write. (Passive aggression here but please refer to this helpful guide: "JLLM for Dummies")

  2. Use chat memory. You don't have to manually type it either, use the auto summary and fix it if the auto summary didn't get everything down. Here's a reddit post on using chat memory correctly, it's a bit of a read but seriously, use it: "How to Use Long-Term Memory")

  3. Use the advanced prompt menu. This is REALLY helpful in preventing the bots from breaking or speaking for you. You can manually type in your own advanced prompt but I use kolach3's prompts.

  4. Change the Temperature settings. I don't have a recommended setting but I mainly use 1.15 to 0.95 depending on the bot. 1.15 or more is
creator: Joosu
